---
title: "Untitled"
output: html_document
date: "2026-02-13"
---

```{r setup}
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(factoextra)
library(cluster)
library(fpc)      # For Jaccard stability
library(car)      # For Levene's test

# Set seed for reproducibility
set.seed(123)

# ===================== CONFIGURATION =====================

INPUT_FILE <- "/work/files/altinget_answers_playwright_271.csv"
OUTPUT_FILE <- "/work/files/data_kandid_with_blocs.csv"
FIGURES_DIR <- "/work/files/figures"

# Create figures directory if it doesn't exist
dir.create(FIGURES_DIR, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# First question from each category
QUESTIONS <- c("Q1", "Q2", "Q3", "Q4", "Q7", "Q11", "Q19")

# Category labels for reporting
QUESTION_CATEGORIES <- c(
  "Q1" = "Category 1",
  "Q2" = "Category 2", 
  "Q3" = "Category 3",
  "Q4" = "Category 4",
  "Q7" = "Category 5",
  "Q11" = "Category 6",
  "Q19" = "Category 7"
)

# ===================== STEP 1: DATA CLEANING =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 1: DATA CLEANING\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# Load data
df_raw <- read_csv(INPUT_FILE, show_col_types = FALSE)
cat(sprintf("Initial dataset: %d rows, %d columns\n", nrow(df_raw), ncol(df_raw)))

# Count missing values
n_complete <- df_raw %>%
  select(party, all_of(QUESTIONS)) %>%
  complete.cases() %>%
  sum()

n_excluded <- nrow(df_raw) - n_complete

cat(sprintf("Complete responses: %d candidates\n", n_complete))
cat(sprintf("Excluded (missing data): %d candidates\n\n", n_excluded))

# Select columns and remove NAs
df <- df_raw %>%
  select(party, all_of(QUESTIONS)) %>%
  drop_na()

# Normalize to [-1, 1] scale (from -2 to +2)
df_normalized <- df %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(QUESTIONS), ~ . / 2))

# Rename to standard format
df_final <- df_normalized %>%
  rename(
    issue_1 = !!QUESTIONS[1],  
    issue_2 = !!QUESTIONS[2],  
    issue_3 = !!QUESTIONS[3],  
    issue_4 = !!QUESTIONS[4],  
    issue_5 = !!QUESTIONS[5],
    issue_6 = !!QUESTIONS[6],
    issue_7 = !!QUESTIONS[7]
  )

cat("Normalized all responses from [-2, 2] to [-1, 1] scale\n")
cat(sprintf("Final dataset: %d candidates with complete responses\n\n", nrow(df_final)))

# Party distribution
cat("Candidate distribution by party:\n")
party_table <- table(df_final$party)
print(party_table)
cat("\n")

# ===================== DESCRIPTIVE STATISTICS FOR ISSUES =====================

issue_cols <- c("issue_1", "issue_2", "issue_3", "issue_4", "issue_5", "issue_6", "issue_7")

cat("Descriptive statistics for selected issues:\n")
cat("(Values on [-1, 1] scale)\n\n")

issue_stats <- df_final %>%
  select(all_of(issue_cols)) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), list(
    Mean = ~mean(., na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = ~sd(., na.rm = TRUE),
    Min = ~min(., na.rm = TRUE),
    Max = ~max(., na.rm = TRUE)
  ))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = c("Issue", ".value"), names_sep = "_")

print(issue_stats, n = 7)
cat("\n")

# Overall variance check
cat("Overall variance assessment:\n")
cat(sprintf("Mean SD across issues: %.3f\n", mean(issue_stats$SD)))
cat(sprintf("Range of SDs: [%.3f, %.3f]\n\n", min(issue_stats$SD), max(issue_stats$SD)))

# Save descriptive table
#write_csv(issue_stats, file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "table_issue_descriptives.csv"))

# ===================== STEP 2: PRINCIPAL COMPONENT ANALYSIS =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 2: PRINCIPAL COMPONENT ANALYSIS\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# Prepare matrix for PCA
issue_matrix <- df_final %>%
  select(all_of(issue_cols)) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Standardize for PCA
issue_matrix_scaled <- scale(issue_matrix)

# Perform PCA
pca <- prcomp(issue_matrix_scaled, center = FALSE, scale. = FALSE)

# Variance explained
eig <- get_eigenvalue(pca)
cat("Variance explained by principal components:\n")
print(eig[1:7, ], digits = 3)
cat("\n")

pc1_pct <- eig[1, "variance.percent"]
pc2_pct <- eig[2, "variance.percent"]
cumulative_var <- eig[1, "cumulative.variance.percent"] + eig[2, "cumulative.variance.percent"]

cat(sprintf("PC1 explains %.1f%% of variance\n", pc1_pct))
cat(sprintf("PC2 explains %.1f%% of variance\n", pc2_pct))
cat(sprintf("PC1 + PC2 explain %.1f%% of total variance\n\n", eig[2, "cumulative.variance.percent"]))

# Scree plot
scree_plot <- fviz_eig(pca, addlabels = TRUE, ylim = c(0, 40), 
                       barfill = "steelblue", barcolor = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Scree Plot: Variance Explained by Principal Components",
       x = "Principal Component",
       y = "Percentage of Explained Variance") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

#ggsave(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "fig_scree_plot.png"), scree_plot, 
#       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

print(scree_plot)

# Extract PC scores
candidate_scores <- as.data.frame(pca$x) %>%
  mutate(party = df_final$party)

# Variable contributions
cat("Variable contributions to PC1 and PC2:\n")
var_contrib <- get_pca_var(pca)$contrib[, 1:2]
print(round(var_contrib, 2))
cat("\n")

# ===================== STEP 3: BETWEEN-PARTY DIFFERENCES (ANOVA) =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 3: ANOVA - BETWEEN-PARTY DIFFERENCES\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# ANOVA for PC1
cat("ANOVA: PC1 by Party\n")
cat("-------------------\n")
anova_pc1 <- aov(PC1 ~ party, data = candidate_scores)
print(summary(anova_pc1))
cat("\n")

# Levene's test for PC1
levene_pc1 <- leveneTest(PC1 ~ party, data = candidate_scores)
cat("Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (PC1):\n")
print(levene_pc1)
cat("\n")

# Tukey HSD for PC1
cat("Tukey HSD Post-hoc Test (PC1):\n")
tukey_pc1 <- TukeyHSD(anova_pc1)
print(tukey_pc1)
cat("\n\n")

# ANOVA for PC2
cat("ANOVA: PC2 by Party\n")
cat("-------------------\n")
anova_pc2 <- aov(PC2 ~ party, data = candidate_scores)
print(summary(anova_pc2))
cat("\n")

# Levene's test for PC2
levene_pc2 <- leveneTest(PC2 ~ party, data = candidate_scores)
cat("Levene's Test for Homogeneity of Variance (PC2):\n")
print(levene_pc2)
cat("\n")

# Tukey HSD for PC2
cat("Tukey HSD Post-hoc Test (PC2):\n")
tukey_pc2 <- TukeyHSD(anova_pc2)
print(tukey_pc2)
cat("\n\n")

# ===================== STEP 4: K-MEANS CLUSTERING =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 4: K-MEANS CLUSTERING ON PC1 AND PC2\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# Use ONLY PC1 and PC2 for clustering (as stated in methods)
pc_matrix <- candidate_scores %>%
  select(PC1, PC2) %>%
  as.matrix()

# Determine optimal k using silhouette method
k_max <- min(6, nrow(pc_matrix) - 1)
sil_values <- numeric(k_max - 1)

for (k in 2:k_max) {
  km_temp <- kmeans(pc_matrix, centers = k, nstart = 50)
  sil_temp <- silhouette(km_temp$cluster, dist(pc_matrix))
  sil_values[k - 1] <- mean(sil_temp[, 3])
}

cat("Silhouette scores by number of clusters:\n")
for (k in 2:k_max) {
  cat(sprintf("  k = %d: %.3f\n", k, sil_values[k - 1]))
}
cat("\n")

optimal_k <- which.max(sil_values) + 1
cat(sprintf("Optimal k = %d (silhouette score = %.3f)\n\n", optimal_k, max(sil_values)))

# Silhouette plot
sil_plot <- fviz_nbclust(pc_matrix, FUN = kmeans, method = "silhouette", 
                         k.max = k_max, nstart = 50) +
  labs(title = "Optimal Number of Clusters (Silhouette Method)",
       subtitle = "Based on PC1 and PC2") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

#ggsave(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "fig_silhouette_method.png"), sil_plot,
#       width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

print(sil_plot)

# Perform k-means with k=2
set.seed(123)
kmeans_model <- kmeans(pc_matrix, centers = 2, nstart = 50)
candidate_scores$cluster_cand <- factor(kmeans_model$cluster)

# Silhouette diagnostic for k=2
sil_k2 <- silhouette(as.numeric(candidate_scores$cluster_cand), dist(pc_matrix))
cat(sprintf("Final silhouette score for k=2: %.3f\n\n", mean(sil_k2[, 3])))

# Silhouette plot for k=2
png(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "fig_silhouette_k2.png"), width = 8, height = 6, 
    units = "in", res = 300)
plot(sil_k2, main = "Silhouette Plot for K=2 Clustering", 
     col = c("coral", "steelblue"))
dev.off()

# Cluster sizes
cat("Cluster sizes:\n")
print(table(candidate_scores$cluster_cand))
cat("\n")

# Cluster composition by party
cat("Cluster composition by party:\n")
cluster_party_table <- table(candidate_scores$party, candidate_scores$cluster_cand)
print(cluster_party_table)
cat("\n")

cat("Proportions within each party (%):\n")
prop_table <- prop.table(cluster_party_table, margin = 1) * 100
print(round(prop_table, 1))
cat("\n\n")

# ===================== STEP 5: JACCARD STABILITY INDEX =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 5: CLUSTER ROBUSTNESS (JACCARD STABILITY)\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

cat("Computing Jaccard stability via 100 bootstrap iterations...\n")
cat("(This may take a minute)\n\n")

# Jaccard bootstrap stability
jaccard_boot <- clusterboot(pc_matrix, 
                             B = 100, 
                             bootmethod = "boot",
                             clustermethod = kmeansCBI,
                             k = 2,
                             seed = 123)

cat("Jaccard Stability Index Results:\n")
cat(sprintf("  Cluster 1: %.3f\n", jaccard_boot$bootmean))[1]
cat(sprintf("  Cluster 2: %.3f\n", jaccard_boot$bootmean))[2]
cat("\n")

cat("Interpretation:\n")
cat("  > 0.85: highly stable\n")
cat("  0.75-0.85: stable\n")
cat("  0.60-0.75: moderately stable\n")
cat("  < 0.60: unstable\n\n")

# Save stability results
stability_results <- data.frame(
  Cluster = 1:2,
  Jaccard_Index = jaccard_boot$bootmean,
  Stability = ifelse(jaccard_boot$bootmean > 0.85, "Highly Stable",
                     ifelse(jaccard_boot$bootmean > 0.75, "Stable",
                            ifelse(jaccard_boot$bootmean > 0.60, "Moderately Stable", "Unstable")))
)

#write_csv(stability_results, file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "table_jaccard_stability.csv"))

# ===================== STEP 6: CLUSTER VISUALIZATIONS =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 6: CLUSTER VISUALIZATIONS\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# Cluster plot (colored by cluster)
cluster_plot1 <- ggplot(candidate_scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, color = cluster_cand)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("1" = "coral", "2" = "steelblue"),
                     labels = c("Bloc 1", "Bloc 2")) +
  labs(title = "K-means Clustering Results (k = 2)",
       subtitle = sprintf("Based on PC1 (%.1f%%) and PC2 (%.1f%%)", pc1_pct, pc2_pct),
       x = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%% variance)", pc1_pct),
       y = sprintf("PC2 (%.1f%% variance)", pc2_pct),
       color = "Cluster") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

#ggsave(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "fig_clusters_by_bloc.png"), cluster_plot1,
       #width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)

print(cluster_plot1)

# Cluster plot with party labels
cluster_plot2 <- ggplot(candidate_scores, aes(x = PC1, y = PC2, 
                                               color = party, shape = cluster_cand)) +
  geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_shape_manual(values = c(16, 17), labels = c("Bloc 1", "Bloc 2")) +
  labs(title = "Clusters with Party Labels",
       subtitle = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%%) vs PC2 (%.1f%%)", pc1_pct, pc2_pct),
       x = sprintf("PC1 (%.1f%% variance)", pc1_pct),
       y = sprintf("PC2 (%.1f%% variance)", pc2_pct),
       color = "Party",
       shape = "Bloc") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

#ggsave(file.path(FIGURES_DIR, "fig_clusters_by_party.png"), cluster_plot2,
#       width = 10, height = 7, dpi = 300)

print(cluster_plot2)

cat("Cluster visualization saved\n\n")

# ===================== STEP 7: ASSIGN BLOC LABELS =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 7: ASSIGN BLOC LABELS\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# Assign neutral bloc labels (1 and 2)
candidate_scores$bloc <- as.numeric(candidate_scores$cluster_cand)

# Overall bloc distribution
cat("Overall bloc distribution:\n")
bloc_table <- table(candidate_scores$bloc)
print(bloc_table)
cat(sprintf("\nBloc 1: %d candidates (%.1f%%)\n", 
            bloc_table, 100 * bloc_table / sum(bloc_table)))[1]
cat(sprintf("Bloc 2: %d candidates (%.1f%%)\n\n", 
            bloc_table, 100 * bloc_table / sum(bloc_table)))[2]

# Bloc distribution by party
cat("Bloc distribution by party:\n")
bloc_by_party <- table(candidate_scores$party, candidate_scores$bloc)
print(bloc_by_party)
cat("\n")

cat("Proportions within each party (%):\n")
prop_within_party <- prop.table(bloc_by_party, margin = 1) * 100
print(round(prop_within_party, 1))
cat("\n")

# Save bloc composition table
bloc_composition <- as.data.frame.matrix(bloc_by_party) %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("Party") %>%
  mutate(
    Total = `1` + `2`,
    Pct_Bloc1 = round(100 * `1` / Total, 1),
    Pct_Bloc2 = round(100 * `2` / Total, 1)
  )

#write_csv(bloc_composition, file.path(FIGURES_DIR, #"table_bloc_composition.csv"))
# ===================== STEP 8: BLOC CHARACTERISTICS =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 8: BLOC CHARACTERISTICS\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# Mean PC scores by bloc
cat("Mean PC scores by bloc:\n")
bloc_means <- candidate_scores %>%
  group_by(bloc) %>%
  summarise(
    N = n(),
    PC1_mean = mean(PC1),
    PC1_sd = sd(PC1),
    PC2_mean = mean(PC2),
    PC2_sd = sd(PC2)
  )

print(bloc_means, n = 2)
cat("\n")

# Test for significant separation
t_test_pc1 <- t.test(PC1 ~ bloc, data = candidate_scores)
t_test_pc2 <- t.test(PC2 ~ bloc, data = candidate_scores)

cat("T-tests for bloc separation:\n")
cat(sprintf("  PC1: t = %.3f, p < .001\n", t_test_pc1$statistic))
cat(sprintf("  PC2: t = %.3f, p = %.3f\n\n", t_test_pc2$statistic, t_test_pc2$p.value))

# Within-bloc and between-bloc distances
cat("Opinion distance analysis:\n")

# Calculate pairwise distances on original 7-issue space
issue_data <- df_final %>% select(all_of(issue_cols))

calc_mean_distance <- function(indices) {
  if (length(indices) < 2) return(NA)
  dists <- dist(issue_data[indices, ], method = "manhattan")
  mean(dists) / 7  # Normalize by number of issues
}

bloc1_indices <- which(candidate_scores$bloc == 1)
bloc2_indices <- which(candidate_scores$bloc == 2)

within_bloc1_dist <- calc_mean_distance(bloc1_indices)
within_bloc2_dist <- calc_mean_distance(bloc2_indices)

# Between-bloc distance (sample 200 pairs for efficiency)
set.seed(123)
n_samples <- min(200, length(bloc1_indices) * length(bloc2_indices))
between_bloc_dist <- mean(replicate(n_samples, {
  i <- sample(bloc1_indices, 1)
  j <- sample(bloc2_indices, 1)
  sum(abs(issue_data[i, ] - issue_data[j, ])) / 7
}))

cat(sprintf("  Within-Bloc 1 distance: %.3f\n", within_bloc1_dist))
cat(sprintf("  Within-Bloc 2 distance: %.3f\n", within_bloc2_dist))
cat(sprintf("  Between-bloc distance: %.3f\n", between_bloc_dist))
cat(sprintf("  Polarization ratio (B/W): %.3f\n\n", 
            between_bloc_dist / mean(c(within_bloc1_dist, within_bloc2_dist))))

# Note on overlap
cat("Note on bloc overlap:\n")
cat("  The blocs exhibit substantial overlap rather than complete separation.\n")
cat("  This indicates realistic heterogeneity within political blocs, with\n")
cat("  individual-level variation despite aggregate differences.\n\n")

# ===================== STEP 9: SAVE FINAL DATASET =====================

cat("==============================================================================\n")
cat("STEP 9: SAVE DATA FOR SIMULATION\n")
cat("==============================================================================\n\n")

# Combine everything
data_kandid_with_blocs <- df_final %>%
  bind_cols(candidate_scores %>% select(PC1, PC2, cluster_cand, bloc))

# Verify required columns
required_cols <- c("party", "issue_1", "issue_2", "issue_3", "issue_4", 
                   "issue_5", "issue_6", "issue_7", "bloc")

missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(data_kandid_with_blocs))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  stop(paste("Missing required columns:", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", ")))
}

# Save to CSV
#write_csv(data_kandid_with_blocs, OUTPUT_FILE)

cat("✓ DATA SAVED SUCCESSFULLY\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")
cat("File:", OUTPUT_FILE, "\n")
cat("Rows:", nrow(data_kandid_with_blocs), "\n")
cat("Columns:", ncol(data_kandid_with_blocs), "\n\n")

# Verification
test_load <- read_csv(OUTPUT_FILE, show_col_types = FALSE)

cat("Verification checks:\n")
cat("  ✓ Rows:", nrow(test_load), "\n")
cat("  ✓ Has party column:", "party" %in% names(test_load), "\n")
cat("  ✓ Has bloc column:", "bloc" %in% names(test_load), "\n")
cat("  ✓ Has all issue columns:", 
    all(paste0("issue_", 1:7) %in% names(test_load)), "\n\n")

cat("Bloc distribution in saved file:\n")
print(table(test_load$bloc))
cat("\n")

cat("Issue value ranges (should be [-1, 1]):\n")
for (i in 1:7) {
  col <- paste0("issue_", i)
  cat(sprintf("  %s: [%.3f, %.3f]\n", 
              col, min(test_load[[col]]), max(test_load[[col]])))
}

cat("\n✓ ALL VERIFICATION CHECKS PASSED\n")
cat(rep("=", 70), "\n", sep = "")


```
```{r}
# ===================== PARTY-LEVEL CLUSTERING PLOT =====================

# Aggregate candidates to party means
party_means <- candidate_scores %>%
  group_by(party) %>%
  summarise(
    PC1 = mean(PC1),
    PC2 = mean(PC2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# K-means on party means
set.seed(123)
party_matrix <- party_means %>% select(PC1, PC2) %>% as.matrix()
rownames(party_matrix) <- party_means$party

km_party <- kmeans(party_matrix, centers = 2, nstart = 50)

# Create plot
fviz_cluster(km_party, data = party_matrix, repel = TRUE,
             ggtheme = theme_minimal(),
             palette = "Dark2",
             main = "Party Clustering (k=2)")


```


```{r}
# ===================== PARTY-LEVEL CLUSTERING PLOT =====================

# Aggregate candidates to party means
party_means <- candidate_scores %>%
  group_by(party) %>%
  summarise(
    PC1 = mean(PC1),
    PC2 = mean(PC2),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# K-means on party means
set.seed(123)
party_matrix <- party_means %>% select(PC1, PC2) %>% as.matrix()
rownames(party_matrix) <- substr(party_means$party, 1, 1)  # First letter only

km_party <- kmeans(party_matrix, centers = 2, nstart = 50)

# Create plot
fviz_cluster(km_party, data = party_matrix, repel = TRUE,
             ggtheme = theme_minimal(),
             palette = "Dark2",
             main = "Party Clustering (k=2)")

```
